text: Heat acclimatization is the most important intervention to reduce physiological strain and optimize performance in hot conditions.
Heat acclimatization should comprise repeated exercise-heat exposures over 1-2 weeks.
Athletes should initiate competition and training in a euhydrated state and minimize dehydration during exercise in the heat.
Pre-exercise cooling strategies (e.g., cooling vests) can facilitate heat loss or increase heat storage capacity before training or competing in hot conditions.
Additional or longer recovery periods between and during events should be provided for hydration and body cooling when competitions are held in the heat.
The first hot days of the year pose elevated health risks for athletes, and event scheduling should account for this increased vulnerability.
